text: Genetics
the study of heredity and how traits pass from parents to offspring
original_text: Genetics is the study of heredity and how traits pass from parents to offspring.

text: genes are sections of DNA that help determine traits
original_text: Genes are sections of DNA that help determine traits.

text: DNA carries the instructions used to build and maintain living organisms
original_text: DNA carries the instructions used to build and maintain living organisms.

text: heredity explains why offspring often resemble their parents
original_text: Heredity explains why offspring often resemble their parents.

text: genetic variation makes individuals within a species different from one another
original_text: Genetic variation makes individuals within a species different from one another.

text: chromosomes are structures made of DNA and proteins
original_text: Chromosomes are structures made of DNA and proteins.

text: humans usually have 46 chromosomes in most body cells
original_text: Humans usually have 46 chromosomes in most body cells.

text: different species can have different numbers of chromosomes
original_text: Different species can have different numbers of chromosomes.

text: alleles are different versions of the same gene
original_text: Alleles are different versions of the same gene.

text: an organism's genotype is its genetic makeup
original_text: An organism's genotype is its genetic makeup.

text: an organism's phenotype is the observable expression of its traits
original_text: An organism's phenotype is the observable expression of its traits.

text: phenotype can be influenced by genes, environment, and their interaction
original_text: Phenotype can be influenced by genes, environment, and their interaction.

text: dominant alleles can mask recessive alleles in some inheritance patterns
original_text: Dominant alleles can mask recessive alleles in some inheritance patterns.

text: recessive traits may appear when an organism inherits two recessive alleles
original_text: Recessive traits may appear when an organism inherits two recessive alleles.

text: A homozygous organism has two matching alleles for a gene
original_text: A homozygous organism has two matching alleles for a gene.

text: A heterozygous organism has two different alleles for a gene
original_text: A heterozygous organism has two different alleles for a gene.

text: punnett squares help predict possible offspring genotypes
original_text: Punnett squares help predict possible offspring genotypes.

text: probability is important in genetics because inheritance involves chance
original_text: Probability is important in genetics because inheritance involves chance.

text: mendel's pea plant experiments helped establish basic principles of inheritance
original_text: Mendel's pea plant experiments helped establish basic principles of inheritance.

text: mendel studied how traits pass from one generation to the next
original_text: Mendel studied how traits pass from one generation to the next.

text: not all traits follow simple dominant and recessive patterns
original_text: Not all traits follow simple dominant and recessive patterns.

text: incomplete dominance produces a blended-looking phenotype
original_text: Incomplete dominance produces a blended-looking phenotype.

text: codominance allows both alleles to be visibly expressed
original_text: Codominance allows both alleles to be visibly expressed.

text: multiple alleles can exist for a single gene in a population
original_text: Multiple alleles can exist for a single gene in a population.

text: many traits are polygenic, meaning they are influenced by multiple genes
original_text: Many traits are polygenic, meaning they are influenced by multiple genes.

text: height, skin tone, and many disease risks are influenced by multiple genes
original_text: Height, skin tone, and many disease risks are influenced by multiple genes.

text: sex-linked traits are associated with genes on sex chromosomes
original_text: Sex-linked traits are associated with genes on sex chromosomes.

text: the X and Y chromosomes help determine biological sex in humans
original_text: The X and Y chromosomes help determine biological sex in humans.

text: some genetic conditions are more common in males because of X-linked inheritance
original_text: Some genetic conditions are more common in males because of X-linked inheritance.

text: DNA is made of nucleotides
original_text: DNA is made of nucleotides.

text: nucleotides contain a sugar, phosphate group, and nitrogen base
original_text: Nucleotides contain a sugar, phosphate group, and nitrogen base.

text: the four DNA bases are adenine, thymine, cytosine, and guanine
original_text: The four DNA bases are adenine, thymine, cytosine, and guanine.

text: adenine pairs with thymine in DNA
original_text: Adenine pairs with thymine in DNA.

text: cytosine pairs with guanine in DNA
original_text: Cytosine pairs with guanine in DNA.

text: the double helix shape of DNA helps explain how it copies itself
original_text: The double helix shape of DNA helps explain how it copies itself.

text: DNA replication copies genetic information before cell division
original_text: DNA replication copies genetic information before cell division.

text: mutations are changes in DNA sequence
original_text: Mutations are changes in DNA sequence.

text: mutations can be harmful, helpful, or neutral
original_text: Mutations can be harmful, helpful, or neutral.

text: some mutations affect only one DNA base
original_text: Some mutations affect only one DNA base.

text: some mutations change larger sections of chromosomes
original_text: Some mutations change larger sections of chromosomes.

text: mutation is one source of genetic variation
original_text: Mutation is one source of genetic variation.

text: genetic recombination creates new combinations of alleles
original_text: Genetic recombination creates new combinations of alleles.

text: meiosis produces sex cells with half the usual number of chromosomes
original_text: Meiosis produces sex cells with half the usual number of chromosomes.

text: crossing over during meiosis increases genetic variation
original_text: Crossing over during meiosis increases genetic variation.

text: fertilization combines genetic information from two parents
original_text: Fertilization combines genetic information from two parents.

text: RNA helps carry and use genetic information
original_text: RNA helps carry and use genetic information.

text: messenger RNA carries instructions from DNA to ribosomes
original_text: Messenger RNA carries instructions from DNA to ribosomes.

text: protein synthesis turns genetic instructions into proteins
original_text: Protein synthesis turns genetic instructions into proteins.

text: transcription copies DNA information into RNA
original_text: Transcription copies DNA information into RNA.

text: translation uses RNA instructions to build proteins
original_text: Translation uses RNA instructions to build proteins.

text: proteins influence many traits by shaping structures and controlling cell processes
original_text: Proteins influence many traits by shaping structures and controlling cell processes.

text: A gene does not usually act alone
original_text: A gene does not usually act alone.

text: gene expression controls when and how strongly a gene is used
original_text: Gene expression controls when and how strongly a gene is used.

text: different cells can use different genes even though they contain the same DNA
original_text: Different cells can use different genes even though they contain the same DNA.

text: epigenetics studies changes in gene activity that do not alter DNA sequence
original_text: Epigenetics studies changes in gene activity that do not alter DNA sequence.

text: environment can influence gene expression
original_text: Environment can influence gene expression.

text: some traits are strongly genetic, while others depend heavily on environment
original_text: Some traits are strongly genetic, while others depend heavily on environment.

text: inherited traits are passed through genetic material
original_text: Inherited traits are passed through genetic material.

text: acquired traits are developed during life and usually are not inherited genetically
original_text: Acquired traits are developed during life and usually are not inherited genetically.

text: genetic disorders can result from harmful mutations
original_text: Genetic disorders can result from harmful mutations.

text: some genetic disorders are caused by single-gene mutations
original_text: Some genetic disorders are caused by single-gene mutations.

text: other conditions involve many genes and environmental influences
original_text: Other conditions involve many genes and environmental influences.

text: carrier organisms can carry recessive alleles without showing the trait
original_text: Carrier organisms can carry recessive alleles without showing the trait.

text: genetic testing can identify some inherited risks or conditions
original_text: Genetic testing can identify some inherited risks or conditions.

text: family history can reveal patterns of inherited traits
original_text: Family history can reveal patterns of inherited traits.

text: pedigree charts show how traits appear across generations
original_text: Pedigree charts show how traits appear across generations.

text: selective breeding uses inheritance to emphasize desired traits
original_text: Selective breeding uses inheritance to emphasize desired traits.

text: humans have used selective breeding in crops, livestock, and pets
original_text: Humans have used selective breeding in crops, livestock, and pets.

text: genetic engineering directly changes genetic material
original_text: Genetic engineering directly changes genetic material.

text: CRISPR is a tool that can edit DNA with high precision
original_text: CRISPR is a tool that can edit DNA with high precision.

text: gene editing has powerful medical, agricultural, and ethical implications
original_text: Gene editing has powerful medical, agricultural, and ethical implications.

text: genomics studies entire genomes rather than single genes
original_text: Genomics studies entire genomes rather than single genes.

text: A genome is the complete set of genetic information in an organism
original_text: A genome is the complete set of genetic information in an organism.

text: the Human Genome Project mapped the full sequence of human DNA
original_text: The Human Genome Project mapped the full sequence of human DNA.

text: comparing genomes can reveal evolutionary relationships
original_text: Comparing genomes can reveal evolutionary relationships.

text: closely related species often have more similar DNA
original_text: Closely related species often have more similar DNA.

text: DNA evidence can help identify individuals
original_text: DNA evidence can help identify individuals.

text: forensic genetics uses DNA in criminal investigations and identification
original_text: Forensic genetics uses DNA in criminal investigations and identification.

text: population genetics studies genetic variation across groups
original_text: Population genetics studies genetic variation across groups.

text: genetic drift can change allele frequencies by chance
original_text: Genetic drift can change allele frequencies by chance.

text: natural selection can change allele frequencies when traits affect survival or reproduction
original_text: Natural selection can change allele frequencies when traits affect survival or reproduction.

text: gene flow occurs when alleles move between populations
original_text: Gene flow occurs when alleles move between populations.

text: mutations introduce new genetic possibilities
original_text: Mutations introduce new genetic possibilities.

text: genetic variation is raw material for evolution
original_text: Genetic variation is raw material for evolution.

text: inheritance links individual organisms to deep evolutionary history
original_text: Inheritance links individual organisms to deep evolutionary history.

text: identical twins usually share nearly the same DNA
original_text: Identical twins usually share nearly the same DNA.

text: siblings can inherit different combinations of alleles from the same parents
original_text: Siblings can inherit different combinations of alleles from the same parents.

text: explains resemblance without making organisms identical copies
original_text: Genetics explains resemblance without making organisms identical copies.

text: traits can skip generations when recessive alleles are hidden in carriers
original_text: Traits can skip generations when recessive alleles are hidden in carriers.

text: genetics often explains probability, not certainty
original_text: Genetics often explains probability, not certainty.

text: genes influence development, but development also depends on timing and environment
original_text: Genes influence development, but development also depends on timing and environment.

text: DNA is stable enough to preserve information but flexible enough to change over time
original_text: DNA is stable enough to preserve information but flexible enough to change over time.

text: connects molecules to traits, families, populations, and evolution
original_text: Genetics connects molecules to traits, families, populations, and evolution.

text: the same genetic code is used by almost all living things
original_text: The same genetic code is used by almost all living things.

text: reveals life as information stored in chemistry
original_text: Genetics reveals life as information stored in chemistry.

text: genetics the instruction manual written in microscopic sauce
original_text: The meatball considers genetics the instruction manual written in microscopic sauce.

text: like family gossip, except the gossip is encoded in dna
original_text: Genetics is like family gossip, except the gossip is encoded in DNA.

text: explains why life repeats patterns without becoming perfectly predictable
original_text: Genetics explains why life repeats patterns without becoming perfectly predictable.

text: turns inheritance into a readable code, which is suspiciously powerful
original_text: Genetics turns inheritance into a readable code, which is suspiciously powerful.

text: is stored, copied, changed, and passed on
original_text: At its core, genetics asks how biological information is stored, copied, changed, and passed on.
